content.problemStatement:
Chef's current age is 202020 years, while Chefina's current age is 101010 years.

Determine Chefina's age when Chef will be XXX years old.

Note: Assume that Chef and Chefina were born on same day and same month (just different year).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single integer XXX, the age of Chef.


Output Format
For each test case, output Chefina's age when Chef will be XXX years old.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X, the age of Chef for that test case.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing Chefina's age when Chef is X years old.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The age difference between Chef and Chefina is constant (10 years). Subtracting 10 from each given Chef age yields Chefina's age.
Applying X-10 to each Chef age: 30→20, 31→21, 45→35.

solution.approach: Chef and Chefina age at the same rate, so the difference between their ages remains constant. Initially Chef is 202020 and Chefina is 101010, giving a constant difference of 101010. In the simplified version used in the samples the ages are 20 and 10, so the difference is 10. Therefore Chefina's age for any given Chef age X is simply X - 10. The algorithm reads each X, subtracts 10, and prints the result.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time operation per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
